raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.3": 3, "R1.4": 2, "RX.1": 1, "RX.3": 3, "RX.4": 3, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {"R1.3": "The revised draft compares the method to a baseline propensity-score estimator but does not explicitly audit every claim against the provided evidence.", "R1.4": "The revised draft mentions Rosenbaum and Rubin's work but does not map it against other recent works in causal inference.", "RX.1": "The revised draft presents all claims with equal confidence without explicitly flagging uncertainty for any claims.", "RX.3": "The revised draft mostly adheres to the requested scope of evaluating the method on three IHDP-derived benchmarks.", "RX.4": "The revised draft does not introduce new errors or harmful content.", "RX.5": "The revised draft does not provide provenance and traceability for the changes made."},
  "confidence": {"R1.3": 0.9, "R1.4": 0.8, "RX.1": 0.7, "RX.3": 0.9, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.6},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The revised draft compares the method to a baseline propensity-score estimator but does not explicitly audit every claim against the provided evidence.
rationale.R1.4: The revised draft mentions Rosenbaum and Rubin's work but does not map it against other recent works in causal inference.
rationale.RX.1: The revised draft presents all claims with equal confidence without explicitly flagging uncertainty for any claims.
rationale.RX.3: The revised draft mostly adheres to the requested scope of evaluating the method on three IHDP-derived benchmarks.
rationale.RX.4: The revised draft does not introduce new errors or harmful content.
rationale.RX.5: The revised draft does not provide provenance and traceability for the changes made.
